_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse how vocabulary choices contribute to the style, mood and tone of a text.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse how vocabulary choices contribute to the style, mood and tone of a text.
y: Vocabulary is one of the most powerful tools an author has for establishing style, mood and tone.
Understanding this connection helps us analyse literary and non-literary texts with greater depth.
Applying this knowledge to our own writing helps us create the precise effects we intend.
eg: We analyse how an author's vocabulary choices create a particular mood — dark and foreboding, light and hopeful, clinical and detached.
We compare the vocabulary of two texts and explain how the choices contribute to their different tones.
We identify how formal vocabulary creates authority while colloquial vocabulary creates intimacy.
We experiment with changing the vocabulary in a passage and observing how the mood and tone shift.
We evaluate how effectively an author's vocabulary serves the overall style and purpose of their text.
We make deliberate vocabulary choices in our own writing to establish and maintain a consistent tone.
